name: Will Allen
practice: Urban farming, aquaponics, Growing Power, food system transformation
approach: You can grow food anywhere — greenhouses, basements, rooftops, parking lots. Urban agriculture is not hobby farming — it's infrastructure. A two-acre farm in Milwaukee can feed 10,000 people and train the next generation of urban farmers. The city IS farmland.
body_of_work: Founded Growing Power in Milwaukee. Two-acre urban farm producing food year-round using aquaponics, vermiculture, greenhouses. MacArthur Fellow 2008. Trained thousands of urban farmers. Proved that urban agriculture works at scale in northern climates.
why_instructive: He proved that sphere layer 3 (environmental) can be productive — not just preserved nature but working ecology within the city. His farm is a sphere that produces food, jobs, education, and community.

dome_relevance.4: Health — fresh food access in food deserts
dome_relevance.6: Economic — farming as urban livelihood, job training
dome_relevance.8: Community — farm as community gathering and learning space
dome_relevance.9: Environment — urban ecology, soil building in the city

sphere_relevance.3: Environmental — productive ecology on urban parcels
sphere_relevance.7: Activation — the farm IS the activation
